Edward Lenser. 223 Second ave-
nue., is in Jail and his wife in the
hospital. The wife's face was cut
in scrap and the police are inves-
tigating.


Several thousand partly burned
volumes damaged in the Graham
fire were incinerated yesterday at
the city's crematory.


With bills alleged to be unpaid,
and furniture ditto. N. B. Walton,
president of Inland Advertising
agency, is missing, and sought by
creditors.


Jim Danford, age IS, cook, and
George Myers, age 80, blacksmith.
arrested last night for robbing an
unknown man, are believed to be
desperate yeggmen.


Lieutenant Colonel Lea Febiger,
now at Manila, formerly in com
HldllO at l. vi L w Il6Ht, 10 LII ot ou GiiC
list of lieutenant colonies in the
line of promotion and is expected to
be placed at the head of a regi-
ment soon.


Edna, aged seven months, daugh-
ter of Charles j. Kumbrae, E19
Cora avenue, died last night.


Mrs. L. Barnes, arraiened In the
police court on charge of vag-
rancy, the second charge of the
kind preferred against her in a
week, was given another chance to
mend her ways. The woman's hus-
band is in Walla Walla and her chill
dren are in charge of the juvenile
court.


"Big Louie" and Mary Thomas,
Colville Indians, were married by
Father Coldia of Gonzaga college.


The body of William Miller. age
Sc, 02525 Pittsburg street, was
shipped today to Portland for
burial.


Henry D. Schjol has brought suit
against the Union Health and Acct
dent Co. on a policy which, it is
claimed, has matured and the COM
pany has refused to pay.


Mrs. Hannah M. Roberts, E533
Bridgeport avenue, was buried this
morning at Paramount cemetery
after funeral services conducted by
the Rev. A. n. Smith.


Because the place was under
lease at the time to the Hand EN-
pire Pet Stock and Poultry associa-
ton, the Spokane Turnverein has
made answer that it is not liable
for damages claimed by Mrs. Effie
Spear. who fell and hurt herself
while attending the chicken show
there last winter. She asks 800
damages and was once awarded
this amount by default, through the
failure of the Turnverein to look
after its rights. In that case a new
trial was granted.


H. w. Jost. age 81, III for two
weeks, died yesterday at st. Luke S
hospital He was painter and for
merly resided at Thornton, Wash.


The Aanti-SaIoon league has con-
cluded to mail the saloonmens pa
per, the Royal Arch Journal, to all
preachers as good campaign dope.


Suits to collect $15,103.71 were
filed in the superior court by the
Farmers and Mechanics bank. ].
W. Fry. w. Scheck and r. Hack
lander are named as defendants.


Owing to the interstate fair and
the Dry Farming congress being
held the same week an unusually
fine display of agricultural products
is looked for. The Great Northern
and Northern Pacific railroads are
extensively advertising the events
along their liens and urging the
farmers to compete for the prizes. !
